To solve the problem, you can use the information that the man's original speed was increased by 3 km/h to obtain a 40-minute reduction in time, and decreased by 2 km/h to obtain a 40-minute increase in time.

Let the original speed be x km/h. Let the distance be d km. Time taken at original speed is d / x hours.

Speed increased by 3 km/h is x + 3 km/h. Time taken at this speed is d / (x + 3) hours.

Time reduced by 40 minutes is d / (x + 3) hours - 2/3 hours.
Time increased by 40 minutes is d / (x - 2) hours + 2/3 hours.

Equating both expressions for time, you get:

$$\frac{d}{x+3}-\frac{2}{3}=\frac{d}{x-2}+\frac{2}{3}$$Simplifying, you get:

$$d=40\;\;\mathrm{km}$$

The correct distance is 40 km.
